reviewer: AAI Code Review (independent context, model claude-fable-5)

acs.Spec-AC-03: NON-COMPLIANT (prototype-chain field names bypass the closed whitelist and write)

findings.message: resolveClearList membership test uses plain property access (spec[f]) on an object literal, so inherited Object.prototype names (toString, __proto__, constructor, valueOf, hasOwnProperty, isPrototypeOf, ...) pass the closed whitelist on all four --clear subcommands: exit 0 and a junk `field: null` line is written into the STATE block; check-state accepts the polluted file. Violates Spec-AC-03 (unknown field must exit 2 with no write) and the engine's exit-2-before-any-write contract. Verified: no status/verdict/policy field is reachable through this hole (not prototype members) — decision logic stays safe, the strict-validation invariant does not.
findings.evidence: probes: set-worktree --clear toString|__proto__|constructor -> exit 0, junk key written; set-validation --clear valueOf|hasOwnProperty|isPrototypeOf -> exit 0; set-validation --clear status -> exit 2 (control); check-state exit 0 on polluted file
findings.recommendation: const info = Object.hasOwn(spec, f) ? spec[f] : undefined; (or hasOwnProperty.call / Object.create(null) maps); add --clear toString / --clear __proto__ refusal cases to test_029

findings.message: Repeated --clear flags are last-wins ('clear' not in MULTI_FLAGS): `set-worktree --clear branch --clear path` exits 0 with branch left stale — a whole clear instruction silently dropped, the exact silent-drop class the W5 hardening targets and the stale-leak class F1 exists to fix.
findings.evidence: probe: set-worktree --clear branch --clear path -> exit 0, path: null, branch: feat/stale survives
findings.recommendation: add 'clear' to MULTI_FLAGS and merge comma-lists in resolveClearList, or refuse repeated --clear with exit 2

findings.message: fieldSpan stops at the first blank line, so clearing (or setField-overwriting) a hand-edited `>-` field whose content contains a blank line orphans the post-blank continuation lines: notes becomes the junk STRING 'null\npara two line' (PyYAML-confirmed), not null; check-state exit 0 hides it. Pre-existing helper class (W1-of-SPEC-0012 family) shared with every setField overwrite; --clear widens the reachable surface. Engine-written `>-` fields are safe (textFieldLines never emits blank lines).
findings.evidence: probe: code_review.notes >- with two blank-separated paragraphs; set-code-review --clear notes -> notes: null + orphaned continuation; yaml.safe_load -> 'null\npara two line'
findings.recommendation: make fieldSpan span blank lines while the next line is more-indented than the field, or refuse (exit 1) to rewrite a field whose span is followed by a deeper-indented post-blank continuation

findings.message: Live STATE's CHANGE-0008 item still carries the legacy stranded spec_path after the blank line; the fixed writer updates it in place by design and never re-normalizes. One-time manual normalization would retire the last legacy shape.
findings.recommendation: optional: move the spec_path line above the blank during closeout

findings.message: Only --clear status gets the sanctioned-path hint (reset-block); required/recommendation/user_decision/run_at_utc refusals fall back to the generic unknown-field message. Spec-compliant; naming the flag-only path would be friendlier.

probes.duplicate_spec_path_hunt: clean — legacy stranded spec_path updates in place (count 1); two-item no bleed; last-block-no-trailing-blank correct + idempotent
probes.folded_scalar_clear_hunt: engine-written >- fields clean; hand-edited blank-line paragraphs orphan (W2)
probes.guard_bypass: status/required/recommendation/user_decision/run_at_utc all refused exit 2; reset-block D6 pass-guard intact
probes.suites: test-aai-state.sh 33/33 exit 0; test-aai-hygiene-pack.sh 11/11 exit 0; docs-audit --check --strict --no-event CLEAN exit 0; aai-reap-tests reaped 0
